mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates weakening labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, which directly translates into lower corporate earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 60% of periods where PAYEMS shows a sustained decline.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) beyond a critical threshold signals a severe economic contraction and heightened recessionary fears, leading to significant negative S&P 500 forward returns as investors price in prolonged weakness.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE reached 8.1% in Feb 2009, up from 7.6% in Jan
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in more than 50% of triggered instances where UNRATE rises sharply.

mechanism_narrative: Extremely low federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) combined with persistently falling retail sales (RSAFS) indicates a demand-side liquidity trap or severe economic contraction where monetary policy is ineffective, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
regime_hint: zero lower bound or liquidity trap
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 50% of triggered instances when FEDFUNDS is near zero and RSAFS is declining.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of severe economic contraction, a stabilization or slight rebound in housing starts (HOUST) can signal the bottoming of the housing market and broader economic cycle, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns as recovery expectations build.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month average stabilizes or increases after a significant decline
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 466k in Jan 2009, near historical lows
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in more than 50% of triggered instances during identified bottoming regimes for HOUST.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects weakening manufacturing activity and reduced corporate output, which negatively impacts corporate earnings and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO declined significantly in late 2008 and early 2009
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in more than 60% of periods where INDPRO is declining.

mechanism_narrative: A significant deceleration in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) below a certain level, especially during a recession, signals disinflationary pressures that could lead to further monetary easing or reduced corporate pricing power, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 50% of triggered instances when inflation is very low or negative.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of falling retail sales (RSAFS) and rising unemployment (UNRATE) indicates a severe and broad-based contraction in consumer demand and labor market health, leading to significant negative S&P 500 forward returns as economic outlook deteriorates.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS declined sharply in late 2008 and early 2009
fact:UNRATE rose significantly in late 2008 and early 2009
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in more than 50% of triggered instances where both retail sales are falling and unemployment is rising.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of extreme monetary easing (very low FEDFUNDS), any positive surprise in GDP growth (GDPC1) can signal a turning point and lead to strong positive S&P 500 forward returns, as the economy responds to stimulus and recovery begins.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 0.5% AND GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth turns positive after negative quarters
regime_hint: post-recessionary recovery with accommodative monetary policy
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or GDPC1 turning positive does not correlate with positive returns.
